core_related_work_shortlist.title: Revisiting Kernel Temporal Segmentation as an Adaptive Tokenizer for Long-form Video Understanding
core_related_work_shortlist.authors: Mohamed Afham, Satya Narayan Shukla, Omid Poursaeed, Pengchuan Zhang, Ashish Shah, Sernam Lim
core_related_work_shortlist.exact_reason: Closest published prior work to STITCH: task-agnostic, unsupervised, KTS-based adaptive tokenizer over frozen features. STITCH must explicitly differentiate from this paper.
core_related_work_shortlist.summary: Re-introduces Kernel Temporal Segmentation (KTS) as an unsupervised, task-agnostic, scalable tokenizer for long videos. Operates on pre-extracted frozen features and produces variable-length semantically-consistent segments via change-point detection. Evaluated on long-form video understanding tasks including video classification and temporal action localization.
core_related_work_shortlist.key_difference: STITCH (1) uses a cosine kernel rather than RBF, (2) adds an adaptive chunk-partitioning post-processing step, (3) extends evaluation across GEBD, moment retrieval, and VLM frame selection — a strictly larger task scope, and (4) builds on a stronger frozen backbone (InternVideo2). KTS Revisited only evaluates on classification and TAL.
core_related_work_shortlist.omission_risk: High. Reviewers will likely flag STITCH as 'KTS Revisited++' if this paper is not cited and differentiated.

core_related_work_shortlist.title: TAG: A Simple Yet Effective Temporal-Aware Approach for Zero-Shot Video Temporal Grounding
core_related_work_shortlist.authors: Jin-Seop Lee, SungJoon Lee, Jaehan Ahn, YunSeok Choi, Jee-Hyong Lee
core_related_work_shortlist.exact_reason: Methodologically the closest training-free moment retrieval method to STITCH — uses temporal coherence clustering to combat 'semantic fragmentation', which is essentially the problem STITCH's chunk-based formulation also solves.
core_related_work_shortlist.summary: Zero-shot training-free VTG approach that performs temporal pooling, temporal coherence clustering, and similarity adjustment over frozen VLM features. Achieves SOTA on Charades-STA and ActivityNet without any task-specific training. Explicitly identifies and addresses semantic fragmentation across temporally continuous frames.
core_related_work_shortlist.placement: related work prose, experiments section context
core_related_work_shortlist.key_difference: TAG uses k-means-style coherence clustering scored against the query at inference; STITCH uses kernel CPD which produces a query-agnostic segmentation that can be reused. TAG is moment-retrieval-only; STITCH applies the same segmentation across GEBD, moment retrieval, and VLM frame selection.
core_related_work_shortlist.omission_risk: High. Direct training-free moment retrieval competitor on the same benchmarks; missing this would be a clear citation gap.

core_related_work_shortlist.title: Training-free Video Temporal Grounding using Large-scale Pre-trained Models (TFVTG)
core_related_work_shortlist.authors: Minghang Zheng, Xinhao Cai, Qingchao Chen, Yuxin Peng, Yang Liu
core_related_work_shortlist.exact_reason: Established training-free VTG baseline; widely cited in the training-free temporal grounding literature; expected baseline in STITCH Table 2.
core_related_work_shortlist.summary: Training-free temporal video grounding approach that enumerates proposals and uses large pretrained VLMs (BLIP-2, GPT-style) to score them. Combines dynamic query-aware scoring with static temporal priors. Evaluated on Charades-STA and ActivityNet Captions.
core_related_work_shortlist.placement: related work prose, experiments section context
core_related_work_shortlist.key_difference: TFVTG enumerates candidate proposals at fixed strides and scores them with a heavy LLM/VLM pipeline. STITCH segments adaptively, has no LLM dependency at inference, and produces reusable chunks across queries.
core_related_work_shortlist.omission_risk: High. This is the canonical training-free VTG baseline.

core_related_work_shortlist.title: Zero-shot Video Moment Retrieval via Off-the-shelf Multimodal Large Language Models (Moment-GPT)
core_related_work_shortlist.authors: Yifang Xu et al. (Nanjing University)
core_related_work_shortlist.exact_reason: Zero-shot training-free moment retrieval baseline using frozen MLLMs; expected baseline alongside TFVTG.
core_related_work_shortlist.summary: Tuning-free pipeline for zero-shot VMR. Uses LLaMA-3 to correct/rephrase the query and frozen MLLMs to score temporal spans. Identifies and corrects language bias as a contribution.
core_related_work_shortlist.key_difference: Moment-GPT is heavyweight (LLaMA-3 + MLLMs at inference per query). STITCH precomputes chunks once per video and runs lightweight scoring per query.
core_related_work_shortlist.omission_risk: Medium. Direct training-free baseline but the LLM-heavy variant is one of several similar approaches.

core_related_work_shortlist.title: Zero-Shot Video Moment Retrieval from Frozen Vision-Language Models
core_related_work_shortlist.authors: Dezhao Luo, Jiabo Huang, Shaogang Gong, Hailin Jin, Yang Liu
core_related_work_shortlist.exact_reason: Earliest zero-shot frozen-VLM moment retrieval baseline; foundational reference for the training-free moment retrieval direction.
core_related_work_shortlist.summary: Argues that training on limited moment-text data yields biased correlations and proposes leveraging frozen VLMs directly without fine-tuning. Uses VLM-based scoring of candidate moments at inference.
core_related_work_shortlist.placement: related work prose, experiments section context
core_related_work_shortlist.key_difference: STITCH does not enumerate fixed proposals; it produces adaptive chunks via CPD. STITCH also reuses these chunks across moment retrieval, GEBD, and VLM reasoning, while this paper is moment-retrieval-only.
core_related_work_shortlist.omission_risk: Medium-high. Established frozen-VLM baseline.

core_related_work_shortlist.title: Point to Span: Zero-Shot Moment Retrieval for Navigating Unseen Hour-Long Videos
core_related_work_shortlist.authors: Mingyu Jeon et al. (Chung-Ang University + KAIST + ETRI)
core_related_work_shortlist.exact_reason: Most directly comparable training-free + long-video + moment retrieval competitor — sits at the intersection of all three STITCH axes.
core_related_work_shortlist.summary: Zero-shot Long Video Moment Retrieval (ZLVMR) for hour-long videos. Adopts a Search-then-Refine paradigm: rapidly narrow candidates, then carefully refine. Argues that processing entire long videos in one pass is computationally infeasible and that staged retrieval is needed.
core_related_work_shortlist.placement: related work prose, experiments section context
core_related_work_shortlist.key_difference: Point-to-Span is search-then-refine and query-driven; STITCH is segment-then-score with query-agnostic structure. The compute model and the temporal-unit granularity differ.
core_related_work_shortlist.omission_risk: High. Direct competitor for STITCH's combination of training-free + long-video moment retrieval.

core_related_work_shortlist.title: Wavelet-based Frame Selection by Detecting Semantic Boundary for Long Video Understanding (WFS-SB)
core_related_work_shortlist.authors: Wang Chen et al. (Xiamen University)
core_related_work_shortlist.exact_reason: Closest published method to STITCH App 3: training-free, query-agnostic, semantic-boundary-based frame selection for long-video VLMs.
core_related_work_shortlist.summary: Argues that current frame selection methods rely on per-frame query relevance and miss narrative structure. Proposes WFS-SB: a training-free framework that uses wavelet analysis to detect semantic shifts (narrative-change moments) and selects frames around these boundaries for downstream LVLM input.
core_related_work_shortlist.placement: related work prose, experiments section context
core_related_work_shortlist.key_difference: WFS-SB uses wavelet-domain analysis on per-frame features; STITCH uses kernel CPD on richer InternVideo2 clip embeddings. The high-level philosophy is identical (boundary-driven, training-free, query-agnostic), so STITCH must distinguish on signal quality, computational cost, and downstream task breadth.
core_related_work_shortlist.omission_risk: High. The closest training-free competitor on the App 3 axis.

core_related_work_shortlist.title: VideoTree: Adaptive Tree-based Video Representation for LLM Reasoning on Long Videos
core_related_work_shortlist.authors: Ziyang Wang, Shoubin Yu, Elias Stengel-Eskin, Jaehong Yoon, Feng Cheng, Gedas Bertasius, Mohit Bansal
core_related_work_shortlist.exact_reason: Training-free, hierarchical, long-video VLM reasoning framework — closest training-free competitor for STITCH App 3 reasoning numbers.
core_related_work_shortlist.summary: Builds a query-adaptive hierarchical tree representation over long videos for LLM reasoning. Iteratively refines keyframe selection based on query relevance; multi-granularity coarse-to-fine traversal.
core_related_work_shortlist.placement: related work prose, experiments section context
core_related_work_shortlist.key_difference: VideoTree is query-adaptive at the structure-building stage (tree depends on query), so its work is rerun per query. STITCH builds query-agnostic chunks once per video and reuses them, which is a different efficiency trade-off.
core_related_work_shortlist.omission_risk: High. Common Table 3 baseline for training-free long-video reasoning.

core_related_work_shortlist.title: Moment Sampling in Video LLMs for Long-Form Video QA
core_related_work_shortlist.authors: Mustafa Chasmai, Gauri Jagatap, Gouthaman KV, Grant Van Horn, Subhransu Maji, Andrea Fanelli
core_related_work_shortlist.exact_reason: Training-free, query-agnostic moment sampling for Video LLMs on long-form QA — directly matches STITCH App 3.
core_related_work_shortlist.summary: Argues uniform sub-sampling for long videos is suboptimal. Proposes a moment-based sampling strategy that improves Video LLM long-form QA performance without training.
core_related_work_shortlist.placement: experiments section context, related work prose
core_related_work_shortlist.key_difference: STITCH's chunks are derived from semantic CPD on InternVideo2 features; specifics of how this paper produces moments differ but the axis is the same.
core_related_work_shortlist.omission_risk: High. Direct App 3 competitor.

core_related_work_shortlist.title: Generic Event Boundary Detection: A Benchmark for Event Segmentation
core_related_work_shortlist.authors: Mike Zheng Shou, Stan Weixian Lei, Weiyao Wang, Deepti Ghadiyaram, Matt Feiszli
core_related_work_shortlist.exact_reason: Defines the GEBD task and Kinetics-GEBD benchmark used in STITCH Table 1.
core_related_work_shortlist.summary: Introduces the Generic Event Boundary Detection task, motivated by cognitive-science findings on natural human video segmentation. Provides Kinetics-GEBD with consistent annotations and Rel.Dis. / F1 metrics.
core_related_work_shortlist.placement: method section context, experiments section context
core_related_work_shortlist.key_difference: STITCH applies its query-agnostic CPD directly to GEBD without supervision; the original benchmark paper expected supervised approaches.
core_related_work_shortlist.omission_risk: Critical (must cite — defines the benchmark).

core_related_work_shortlist.title: QVHighlights: Detecting Moments and Highlights in Videos via Natural Language Queries
core_related_work_shortlist.authors: Jie Lei, Tamara L. Berg, Mohit Bansal
core_related_work_shortlist.exact_reason: STITCH Table 2 evaluation benchmark; also introduces Moment-DETR baseline.
core_related_work_shortlist.summary: 10K+ YouTube videos with moment localization and highlight detection annotations. Introduces Moment-DETR as a transformer-based moment retrieval baseline.
core_related_work_shortlist.key_difference: n/a (benchmark paper).
core_related_work_shortlist.omission_risk: Critical (must cite — defines the benchmark and Moment-DETR baseline).

core_related_work_shortlist.title: Dense-Captioning Events in Videos (ActivityNet Captions)
core_related_work_shortlist.authors: Ranjay Krishna, Kenji Hata, Frederic Ren, Li Fei-Fei, Juan Carlos Niebles
core_related_work_shortlist.exact_reason: Source paper for ActivityNet Captions, the second STITCH Table 2 dataset.
core_related_work_shortlist.summary: Proposes the dense-captioning task and provides annotations on ActivityNet videos. The temporal segments + captions enable language-conditioned moment retrieval.
core_related_work_shortlist.key_difference: n/a (dataset paper).
core_related_work_shortlist.omission_risk: Critical (must cite — defines the second moment retrieval benchmark).

core_related_work_shortlist.title: Video-MME: The First-Ever Comprehensive Evaluation Benchmark of Multi-modal LLMs in Video Analysis
core_related_work_shortlist.authors: Chaoyou Fu et al.
core_related_work_shortlist.exact_reason: STITCH Table 3 long-video VLM reasoning benchmark.
core_related_work_shortlist.summary: Comprehensive evaluation benchmark for video MLLMs spanning short/medium/long video splits with diverse QA categories.
core_related_work_shortlist.key_difference: n/a (benchmark paper).
core_related_work_shortlist.omission_risk: Critical (must cite).

core_related_work_shortlist.title: MLVU: Benchmarking Multi-task Long Video Understanding
core_related_work_shortlist.authors: Junjie Zhou, Yan Shu, Bo Zhao, et al.
core_related_work_shortlist.exact_reason: STITCH Table 3 long-video VLM reasoning benchmark.
core_related_work_shortlist.summary: Multi-task long video understanding benchmark covering needle-in-a-haystack, summarization, topic reasoning, and other subtasks at varying lengths.
core_related_work_shortlist.key_difference: n/a (benchmark paper).
core_related_work_shortlist.omission_risk: Critical (must cite).

core_related_work_shortlist.title: InternVideo2: Scaling Foundation Models for Multimodal Video Understanding
core_related_work_shortlist.authors: Yi Wang et al. (OpenGVLab Shanghai AI Lab)
core_related_work_shortlist.exact_reason: STITCH's frozen backbone — every per-clip embedding comes from InternVideo2.
core_related_work_shortlist.summary: Multi-modal video foundation model trained at scale with multi-modal contrastive + masked objectives. Strong temporal understanding and good downstream transfer.
core_related_work_shortlist.key_difference: n/a (used as a frozen module).
core_related_work_shortlist.omission_risk: Critical (must cite — STITCH builds directly on this).

core_related_work_shortlist.title: Optimal Detection of Changepoints with a Linear Computational Cost (PELT)
core_related_work_shortlist.authors: Rebecca Killick, Paul Fearnhead, Idris A. Eckley
core_related_work_shortlist.exact_reason: PELT is the algorithmic foundation that gives STITCH's segmentation O(n) cost — directly cited in the method section.
core_related_work_shortlist.summary: Introduces PELT: an exact pruning-based dynamic-programming algorithm for offline change-point detection with linear time complexity under standard regularity conditions.
core_related_work_shortlist.key_difference: n/a (algorithmic component).
core_related_work_shortlist.omission_risk: High (must cite if STITCH claims linear-time CPD).

core_related_work_shortlist.title: TimeLoc: A Unified End-to-End Framework for Precise Timestamp Localization in Long Videos
core_related_work_shortlist.authors: Chen-Lin Zhang, Lin Sui, Shuming Liu, Fangzhou Mu, Zhangcheng Wang, Bernard Ghanem
core_related_work_shortlist.exact_reason: Closest published multi-task temporal localization analog to STITCH — explicitly unifies TAL, temporal video grounding, moment retrieval, AND GEBD.
core_related_work_shortlist.summary: Supervised one-stage end-to-end framework that handles temporal action localization, moment retrieval, temporal video grounding, and GEBD in a unified setup. Argues against task-specific models.
core_related_work_shortlist.key_difference: TimeLoc is supervised and trained per-task; STITCH is training-free and produces a query-agnostic segmentation reusable across tasks. TimeLoc is the closest 'unified-but-trained' analog and is the natural anchor for STITCH's multi-task framing claim.
core_related_work_shortlist.omission_risk: High. Without citing TimeLoc, STITCH's multi-task framing claim looks unanchored.

core_related_work_shortlist.title: Generic Event Boundary Detection via Denoising Diffusion (DiffGEBD)
core_related_work_shortlist.authors: Jaejun Hwang, Dayoung Gong, Manjin Kim, Minsu Cho
core_related_work_shortlist.exact_reason: Strong supervised GEBD competitor in Table 1; methodologically interesting because it also uses temporal self-similarity.
core_related_work_shortlist.summary: Diffusion-based generative GEBD model. Encodes per-frame change signals via temporal self-similarity, then iteratively denoises proposed boundary positions.
core_related_work_shortlist.key_difference: DiffGEBD is supervised and uses iterative diffusion sampling. STITCH is unsupervised and produces boundaries via deterministic CPD on the same kind of similarity signal.
core_related_work_shortlist.omission_risk: High (Table 1 baseline).

core_related_work_shortlist.title: Query-Dependent Video Representation for Moment Retrieval and Highlight Detection (QD-DETR)
core_related_work_shortlist.authors: WonJun Moon, Sangeek Hyun, SangUk Park, Dongchan Park, Jae-Pil Heo
core_related_work_shortlist.exact_reason: Strong supervised baseline on QVHighlights; representative of query-conditioned DETR-style methods that STITCH compares against.
core_related_work_shortlist.summary: DETR-style architecture for joint moment retrieval and highlight detection on QVHighlights. Conditions encoder representations on the query throughout.
core_related_work_shortlist.key_difference: QD-DETR is fully supervised and conditions the entire forward pass on the query. STITCH is training-free and produces query-agnostic chunks.
core_related_work_shortlist.omission_risk: Medium-high (Table 2 baseline).

core_related_work_shortlist.title: UniVTG: Towards Unified Video-Language Temporal Grounding
core_related_work_shortlist.authors: Kevin Qinghong Lin, Pengchuan Zhang, Joya Chen, et al.
core_related_work_shortlist.exact_reason: Closest supervised analog of STITCH on the moment retrieval side: pre-trains on multiple grounding tasks then fine-tunes.
core_related_work_shortlist.summary: Unifies video temporal grounding across diverse formats (moment retrieval, highlight detection, video summarization) via a single pre-trained model.
core_related_work_shortlist.placement: related work prose, experiments section context
core_related_work_shortlist.key_difference: UniVTG unifies across formats via supervised pretraining; STITCH unifies across GEBD, moment retrieval, and VLM frame selection via training-free CPD on a frozen backbone.
core_related_work_shortlist.omission_risk: Medium-high (closest supervised multi-task grounding work).
